tables.title: Summary of High-Quality CPR Components — Adult vs Child vs Infant
rows: At least 2 in (5 cm), ≤2.4 in (6 cm)
At least 1/3 AP diameter (~2 in / 5 cm)
At least 1/3 AP diameter (~1.5 in / 4 cm)
2 hands on lower half of sternum
1 or 2 hands on lower half of sternum

tables.title: The Five Components of High-Quality Chest Compressions
rows: At least 2 in (5 cm), not >2.4 in (6 cm)
Generates adequate blood flow; avoids injury
Allows the heart to refill between compressions
Blood flow falls rapidly when compressions stop
Over-ventilation reduces venous return and cardiac output
